_note: To Hit data is the real ASL C3 TO HIT TABLE + C4 GUN & AMMO BASIC TO HIT NUMBER MODIFICATIONS, transcribed from the rules card (page after C27). To Kill data is the real C7.31 AP TO KILL TABLE (numbers still being verified). TH/TK numbers are the 2d6 total you must roll AT OR UNDER; higher = easier. C4 modifications are added to the basic To Hit number (positive = easier). The Hit Determination DRM is a die-roll modifier (positive = harder) and is subtracted from the To Hit number. Range-indexed rows are aligned left-to-right with `range_brackets`.

to_hit._note: Basic To Hit number by target type, split German (black column) vs Other (red column). Final TH# = basic[target][nationality_class][band] + weapon_type mod + ammo mod + size mods (<=57mm and <=40mm STACK) - Hit Determination DRM. A To Hit dr of 2 always hits; 12 always misses.

to_kill._note: C7.31 AP TO KILL TABLE (armored target). BASIC TK# is FIXED per gun, keyed by gun-size string = mm + barrel suffix (Normal->'', *->'*', L->'L', LL->'LL'). A basic_tk value is EITHER an int (same for all nationalities) OR an object with per-country values plus an optional 'default' (= all other countries). Lookup checks the firer's nationality first, then 'default'; if neither, the gun is not available to that nationality. Range enters only via the Case D TK# CHANGE modifier (mm bracket x the To-Kill range brackets below; NA=null=cannot kill at that range). Final TK# = BASIC TK# + Case D mod. Target Armor NOT subtracted. Smoke cannot kill. TODO — CIRCLE BACK: add 'Australian' to the nationality dropdown. 88* is tagged Australian, but Australian is not yet a selectable nationality, so 88* currently matches no firer (decide: add Australian, or map to British). Resolved: Grant-gun exception dropped (75 is a single value 14); 152=21 and 150L=28 confirmed. NOT YET HANDLED: MG/ATR special rows, APDS/APCR bonus, UNARMORED TARGET rule, Multiple Hits (3.8).

case_d._note: Case D TK# CHANGE by mm bracket x To-Kill range bracket. null = NA (cannot kill at that range). Code buckets: mm<=25 -> le25; mm>=65 -> ge65; 26-64 -> mid (ASSUMPTION for the 26-36 / 58-64 gaps).
